channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Co-op Survival FPS Frostrail's First Bigger Playtest is Happening Now
item.content:encoded:
Frostrail is an in-development co-op survival FPS that puts you on a steam-powered train in a post-apocalyptic world of endless winter. There’s a playtest that just kicked off yesterday, and you still have a chance to join.

Developer FakeFish and publisher Shiro Unlimited are holding the playtest via Steam from April 16-27th. So, you’re on a steam train, the world is frozen after a cataclysmic event, and well, there are Revenants to take on. These are the undead versions of those that died in the event, and well, they’re pretty relentless, and they’re haunting the frozen wasteland all over. 

You can play Frostrail solo or with others via co-op, explore, scavenge, find supplies, craft equipment, and take on those Remnants with a mix of melee and firearms. Since this is a survival FPS one of the things you'll also have to focus on during your gameplay is to operate and also maintain the train. The train keeps going, you're on it, so you have to make sure that it stays in good shape, fueled up, and ready. 

This is part of what you can experience if you join the playtest. The tutorial and two other zones to explore are included in the test, along with basic train operation and maintenance. Other parts of the survival gameplay loop, particularly early content, will involve learning how to scavenge, recycle, and craft early equipment. Battle against the Revenants and engage in early-game dungeons, where you can walk away with valuable loot.

FakeFish plans to release Frostrail into Early Access later this year. You can head to Steam for more and to request playtest access. If you’re invited, there will also be a friend invite along with yours.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/co-op-survival-fps-frostrails-first-bigger-playtest-is-happening-now-2000137831
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen Previews the Mail System, Coming in their Combat and Progression Update
item.content:encoded:
Last week, Visionary Realms gave us a peek at Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen’s upcoming tropical region, Badia de Cara (and its sharks). Now, we get a new devblog about the big Combat and Progression update, this time on...the mail system?

The team dives right into their new devblog with the question of what a mail system has to do with combat and progression. The mail system is a network that supports a ton of the other features they’re working on. MMO vets will remember when the way to get something to your alt was by mailing it. Well, there’s that and more. 

You can send and trade with other people, even if the other person is offline, or if they're somewhere far off in the world and you don't feel like traveling over to meet. You can transfer stuff between characters, collect items you purchase from the market, and, if needed, receive items from the developers if they issue some sort of reward or compensation in the future.

At the most basic level, it’s a mail system, so you'll get to access your mail, read it, and send or receive items or coin. Keep organized with filters, and functions like selecting messages, organizing them, hiding what you don’t need, and prioritizing what you do, like guild mail. To send or receive items or coin attached to mail, head to a mail courier or mail kiosk in the world, usually in populated areas, like cities and towns.  

In the blog, we can see examples and screenshots of what they have now, with the  notice that any of this is still subject to change.

The new mail system will be included in the public test realm build when that opens, as the team wants this network of functions to work as needed when the full Combat and Progression Update launches.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pantheon-rise-of-the-fallen-previews-the-mail-system-coming-in-their-combat-and-progression-update-2000137830
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
